text.title: Tech Debt Management Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules every debt-management plan must satisfy.

rule.statement: Strategy mapping by debt score: low (< threshold/2) → Boy Scout; medium (threshold .. 2x) → feature-attached; high (>2x threshold) with localized impact → dedicated sprint; high with broad impact (cross-service) → Strangler Fig.
rule.rationale: Each strategy has different overhead; matching to score avoids hammer-everything-with-Strangler-Fig.

rule.statement: Every chosen strategy MUST declare an exit criterion: Boy Scout exits when a file drops below LoC cap; feature-attached when the feature ships; dedicated sprint when its items are closed; Strangler Fig when traffic switches to 100%.
rule.rationale: "We're paying down debt" without exit becomes permanent toil. The exit criterion bounds the work.

rule.statement: For each debt item paid down, a CI / pre-commit gate is added that detects re-introduction of the category. Without a gate, the same debt returns within 3 sprints.
Debt items live as LABELS on feature tickets and as items in the scored register — NEVER as a separate "tech-debt backlog" that nobody owns. Separate backlogs are where debt goes to die.
rule.rationale: Separate backlogs become a dumping ground; co-location with features keeps the work visible.

rule.statement: Each plan item names an owner by email (not team alias). Owner is responsible for the exit criterion + the gate.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; named owner is durable.

rule.statement: When prerequisites hold (scored register present, CI infra, threshold), execute: classify by score → assign strategy → declare exit → wire gate → schedule. Don't skip the gate.
rule.rationale: Gate-less payoff just delays the next debt cycle.

rule.statement: Do NOT use when (a) no scored debt register — produce one first via `debt-scoring-rubric`, (b) single-PR engagement, (c) project < 3 months, (d) org has no debt-payoff appetite — escalate before any artefact work.
rule.rationale: Strategy overhead needs a scored input + organisational will to act.
